title: Wex Dafid

{{Quote|All right, beings. For some of you, I'm your final hurdle. You've got to get by me to get your hands on a real starfighter. I'm here to make sure you don't leave here as TIE meat. We can't spare the ships.|Wex Dafid|<ref name="XW:OSG" />}}
'''Wex "TIE-Die" Dafid''' was a [[Commander/Legends|commander]] in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] who served as [[Starfighter Command (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Starfighter Command's]] tactical [[Training officer/Legends|training officer]] on the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']] during the early [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. During his time as a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], Dafid flew 150 [[Starfighter combat/Legends|combat missions]] for the Alliance, which left him with a three-inch scar on his cheek and a [[Cybernetics/Legends|mechanical prosthetic]] in place of a lost [[Limb/Legends|hand]]. He also gained an understanding of tactical flying, which aided him in his career as an instructor, though his appearance, and intense personality, often intimidated those he trained.

==Biography==
{{Quote|You can get shot just as dead when there's a moron at the helm as when there's a genius.|Wex Dafid|<ref name="XW:OSG" />}}
Wex Dafid served as a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and survived 150 [[Starfighter combat/Legends|combat missions]] before retiring from active duty and taking a position as a [[Training officer/Legends|training officer]].<ref name="SoR">''[[Strongholds of Resistance]]''</ref> Little was known of Wex Dafid's early career with the Alliance among those he trained during the early [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Stories circulating among rookie pilots told that he had ignored practically every rule of combat and was almost [[Death/Legends|killed]] on several occasions. Though these were only stories, his early career left him with a three-inch scar on his cheek, a [[Cybernetics/Legends|mechanical hand]] and the nickname "TIE-Die."<ref name="XW:OSG" />

[[File:Independence.png|thumb|left|250px|Dafid served as tactical training officer aboard the ''Independence''.]]
By [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]],<ref name="1BBY">''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' begins after the [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]] entered service with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], which ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' places in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]. ''X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide'' establishes that the [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|capture of Leia Organa]] occurs at the end of [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]{{'s}} second tour of duty. Organa's capture takes place on 35:3:3 under the [[Great ReSynchronization]] dating system, placing it at the beginning of [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] according to [[Template:YearMathGRS|the following explanation]]. Events in ''X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide'' before the end of the second tour of duty therefore take place in 1 BBY.</ref> Dafid was serving as [[Starfighter Command (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Starfighter Command's]] chief tactical training officer aboard the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']], having the rank of [[Commander/Legends|commander]] and an office near the ship's [[Flight simulator/Legends|simulator]] room. Dafid oversaw the training of many recruits, including the [[Agamarian]]s [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]] and [[Sunnar Jan-lo]], taking them through simulated missions and teaching the lessons they would need to stay alive in combat. Rarely were pilots allowed near a real [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] until they had completed every training scenario to Dafid's satisfaction. Keyan Farlander was a rare exception, entering active duty on [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna's]] orders after achieving near perfect simulator scores, even though Dafid felt the young pilot was not ready. After leaving Dafid's training, pilots often entered service in one of the ''Independence''{{'s}} starfighter [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]], though some later moved to other assignments. In addition to training recruits, Dafid also retrained those pilots who had demonstrated weaknesses at certain skills.<ref name="XW:OSG" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Ready? Who's ever ready? You'll be all right. Keep asking yourself whether or not you're ready yet, and when the answer is yes, that's when you might consider a nice desk job. The end is near.|Wex Dafid to Keyan Farlander|<ref name="XW:OSG" />}}
Dafid was a tough, no-nonsense officer whose early experiences in combat were rumored to have taught him the importance of proper tactical flying. His often intense personality, combined with the three-inch scar on his cheek and his mechanical hand, the strength of which he struggled to gauge, ensured that the young recruits were often intimidated by the man. As he first examined Farlander's class, observing each pilot in turn while muttering to himself, Farlander's first impression was that Dafid was a "nut case." Nevertheless, Dafid was a competent instructor who cared for the wellbeing of those he trained, and was determined not to let them enter combat until they were ready. Every training mission was designed to teach a specific lesson and Dafid did not care how many attempts it took trainees to complete them, as long as they demonstrated that they had fully understood the lessons being taught. He tended to refer to his pilots by their last name, only calling Farlander by his first name after the pilot was promoted from his class. He did have a sense of humor, though the intimidated recruits in his class rarely laughed at his jokes.<ref name="XW:OSG" />

==Behind the scenes==
Wex Dafid was created by [[Rusel DeMaria]] for his book ''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'', which was published on [[June 1]], [[1993]].  The book presented the story of Keyan Farlander,<ref name="XW:OSG" /> the player character in the game [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']], which was released on [[February 28]], 1993.<ref name="XW" /> In the book Dafid serves as the tactical training officer aboard the ''Independence'' while Farlander is undergoing training.<ref name="XW:OSG" /> In the game, however, Farlander's tactical training takes place away from the ''Independence'', at an [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] which serves as a training center, and the scenario briefings are given by General Dodonna.<ref name="XW">[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]</ref>
